title: Decadent Yeasty Irish Duck Stew
description: A hearty Irish stew featuring tender duck, yeasty flavors, and gooey textures, all in a rich and savory broth. This dish is perfect for a cold and rainy day.

ingredients.name: katsuramuki-cut carrots
ingredients.notes: peeled and cut into thin strips

ingredients.name: duck breast
ingredients.notes: skin removed and cut into bite sized pieces

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the duck fat over medium heat until melted and fragrant.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are softened and translucent, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the katsuramuki-cut carrots and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften.
Add the yeast extract, Irish stout, duck breast, potatoes, thyme, sea salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the duck is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the stew from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
Let the stew rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
